questions.question: The primary function of the large intestine is:
Which of the following is NOT a part of the large intestine?
The longitudinal muscle layer of the large intestine forms:
The pouch-like structures in the large intestine is caused by:
Vitamin K is mainly synthesized by:
The large intestine, in adults, is approximately?
Which type of cells in the large intestine are primarily responsible for mucus secretion?
Which anatomical feature characterizes the muscularis externa of the large intestine?
options: Circular smooth muscle layer arranged in folds
Longitudinal muscle arranged in three bands called teniae coli
A single layer of longitudinal smooth muscle

questions.question: What is the primary role of the abundant bacterial flora present in the large intestine?
In the context of the appendix, which of the following statements is true?
options: The appendix has many villi for absorption.
The appendix is larger in diameter than the cecum.
The appendix contains extensive lymphoid follicles.
The muscularis mucosa layer is well-defined throughout the appendix.

questions.question: The large intestine is longer than the small intestine.
The large intestine has many villi for absorption.
Taeniae coli help in the formation of haustra (sacules).
Chemical digestion of food occurs mainly in the large intestine.
The appendix is attached to the cecum.
Appendices epiploicae are fat-filled pouches on the surface of the colon.
